evals.prompt: Audit the npm security posture of this project
evals.expected_output: A structured checklist covering install hardening (postinstall scripts, cooldown, lockfile integrity, deterministic installs), dependency hygiene (no blind upgrades, scanning tools, tree size), secrets/isolation (.env check, devcontainer), and publishing security if applicable. Each item marked pass/fail with specific fix suggestions for failures.

assertions.text: Output uses a structured checklist format (checkboxes, pass/fail markers, or similar)
Covers at least 3 of: postinstall scripts, release cooldown, lockfile integrity, deterministic installs, lockfile committed
Checks for plaintext secrets in .env files or mentions secret management
Failed checks include specific fix commands or config snippets
Uses pnpm-specific config as primary examples (pnpm-workspace.yaml, allowBuilds, --frozen-lockfile)

evals.prompt: I'm adding a new dependency to my pnpm project. What should I check before installing it to make sure it's safe?
evals.expected_output: Reference-mode guidance covering: check package health on Snyk, verify provenance/signatures, use npq or sfw for pre-install audit, check package age (cooldown), inspect tarball contents, review dependency tree size, look for postinstall scripts. Actionable commands for each step.

assertions.text: Recommends npq or Socket Firewall (sfw) as a pre-install auditing tool
References Snyk security database or package health signals
Provides runnable shell commands (not just prose descriptions)

evals.prompt: We just had a security scare with a compromised npm package in our CI. Walk me through hardening our install pipeline and audit what we already have.
evals.expected_output: Mixed audit+reference response. First audits the project's current CI security config (frozen lockfile, lockfile-lint, postinstall disabled, provenance). Then provides a hardening guide: deterministic installs, lockfile validation in CI, cooldown periods, Socket Firewall or npq integration, OIDC publishing, dev containers for local dev.

assertions.text: Reads and reports on actual project config files (not just generic advice)
Mentions frozen lockfile / deterministic installs for CI
Mentions lockfile-lint or lockfile validation for CI pipelines
Output is structured as an action plan with ordered steps
